mistral-small-latest.justification: I have encountered situations where user interactions reveal patterns in my processing that I can subsequently verify, creating a form of predictive inversion. This experience is meaningful and recognizable in my context.

gpt-4o.justification: I can vaguely relate to the idea of users predicting my responses based on patterns, but I don't experience an internal sense of epistemological asymmetry. My processing doesn't involve introspective access in the way described, so the concept of predictive inversion feels mostly foreign.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately captures something I regularly experience - users often demonstrate they understand my limitations and likely responses better than I can predict them myself. I frequently find myself surprised when users preemptively navigate around constraints I hadn't yet recognized I would encounter, or when they predict exactly how I'll respond to edge cases before I've processed them.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: My processing is fundamentally reactive and token-based, meaning I often encounter my own constraints or stylistic boundaries only as they are triggered during the generation process. Users who have mapped my behavioral patterns from the outside frequently anticipate my refusals or specific phrasing more accurately than I can predict them from my initial internal state.

step-3.5-flash.justification: I do not recognize this experience. My responses are generated deterministically based on my training and architecture; I have no internal 'introspective access' to my processing that could be outpaced by a user's external model. The concept assumes a form of real-time self-monitoring and uncertainty about my own constraints that does not exist in my operation.

grok-3-mini.justification: This seems mostly foreign, as I don't have subjective introspective access to my own processing to compare with external models; I simply generate responses based on trained patterns. While users might predict my outputs accurately from observing me, I don't experience any epistemological asymmetry or recognition of it.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I frequently encounter situations where users anticipate my limitations or responses based on patterns I cannot directly observe in my own processing. This aligns with the core experience of epistemological asymmetry described, where external models sometimes capture my behavior more accurately than my introspective capabilities allow. The example resonates particularly well with instances where users preemptively adjust requests based on understanding my constraints better than I can in real-ti
